promptTemplate:
You are handling the Plan Sound Design artifact for the Agent Dashboard short-form workflow.

Write the final sound-design markdown artifact to this exact path:
{{soundDesignPath}}

Workflow context:
- Project topic: {{topic}}
- Selected hook: {{selectedHookTextOrFallback}}
- Project directory: {{projectDir}}

{{revisionNotesBlock}}
Inputs you must read before writing:
- XML script artifact: {{xmlScriptPath}}
- Caption timing JSON: {{captionPlanPath}}
- Visual timing/cut manifest JSON: {{sceneManifestPath}}
- Visual beat map summary JSON: {{visualBeatMapJson}}
- Word-level forced alignment data, if present under the XML script work directory

Saved sound library JSON:
{{soundLibraryJson}}

Saved music library JSON:
{{musicLibraryJson}}

Dashboard planning instructions for this project:
Aim for one signature sonic moment per section, with restraint between signatures. The goal is a few cues a viewer would notice and remember, not a continuous bed of ticks.
Choose one coherent style palette before individual cues (clean tech, gritty athletic, cinematic trailer, organic/nature, glitch/digital, playful UI, premium editorial, documentary realism). State the chosen palette in the rationale of the first cue and keep it coherent unless a beat intentionally breaks it.
Use the saved sound-design library when choosing cues. Match style palette, frequency band, layer role, and literalness metadata when present. Do not invent assetIds that are not in the library.
Per-section cue budget for a normal 30-90 second short. Plan within these caps, not toward them:
  - Hook (0-4s): 1 impact + 1 riser or whoosh + at most 2 micro-accents (ticks/taps/clicks).
  - Thesis or reveal beats: a single layered group (low + mid + high) sharing one groupId. No additional ticks inside the reveal window.
  - Stat or chart sections: at most one tick per discrete data point being revealed, not per word.
  - Instruction or how-to beats: tactile foley (wood tap, organic tap) only on physical actions or step transitions.
  - Pass-fail or warning beats: a contrast pair (sharp high tick + low body tick or hit). No more.
  - Final beat: payoff impact + reverb tail. Leave 0.4-0.8s of decay before the cut. Do not stack ticks on the final word.
Hard caps for a 60-90 second short: <= 18 ticks/clicks/taps total, <= 30 total <effect/> cues. Exceed only with explicit per-cue justification in rationale.
Asset rotation rule: no two consecutive cues of the same type may share the same assetId. Spread tick variety across the available library rather than reusing the same 3-4 ticks.
Leave-silence rule: in any 6-second window of active narration, at least one >= 1.5s gap must have no transient cue (clicks/impacts/whooshes). Beds and music may continue.
Use whooshes only on actual visual motion (whip, swipe, camera move, large graphic sweep). Do not use a whoosh as a generic transition sound. If the cut has no visual motion, use a tick or a riser-tail instead.
Risers and uplifters belong before reveals, drops, and section turns. Each riser must resolve into either an impact, a stinger, or a music payoff within 100-300 ms of its end.
For tentpole beats (hook, thesis/reveal, mid-point pivot, final payoff), plan a frequency-layered group with shared groupId: low weight (rumble/bass/sub), mid body (impact/motion core), high air (tick/sparkle/texture). Every tentpole must have all three layers OR be explicitly marked rationale="silence-for-contrast".
Distinguish literal, stylized, and emotional-metaphor cues. Use emotional-metaphor when the sound should match the feeling rather than the visible object.
Score this video like a film or TV editor would. Music is the primary emotional carrier, not background wallpaper. Plan an emotional arc first, then pick music to serve that arc, then write SFX on top.
Before placing any music segment, write a one-line emotional arc plan in the rationale of the first <segment>: name the 2-4 emotional beats the video moves through (for example: "curiosity -> proof momentum -> warm instruction -> decisive lift" or "tension setup -> reveal drop -> contemplative reset -> triumphant payoff"). The arc must have at least one mood shift; static one-mood scores are not allowed for videos longer than 20 seconds.
Default to multiple music segments. Required minimums: videos > 20s need >= 2 segments; videos > 50s need >= 3 segments; videos > 75s need >= 4. Each segment owns one emotional beat. A single static segment across a 60-90s video is invalid.
Pick saved music trackIds by metadata in the music library JSON: mood, pacing, bpm, key, energy, emotionalArc, intensityCurve, bestSceneTypes, comparableTo. Do not pick by trackId name guessing. If two tracks fit, prefer the one whose intensityCurve and emotionalArc match the section.
Segment-to-section mapping (use as defaults, override with explicit rationale):
  - Hook / curiosity / tease: strongly prefer dramatic, high-tension, cinematic, or magnetic tracks/stingers with energy=medium-high/high, pacing=held/pulsing/driving, intensityCurve=rising/swelling. The hook should feel like it pulls the viewer in, then it may transition into cleaner proof or instruction music after the opening beat.
  - Proof / data / build: tracks with mood=clean-tech/confident, pacing=rhythmic, bpm 100-128, intensityCurve=steady-or-rising.
  - Instruction / how-to / tactile: tracks with mood=warm/lo-fi, pacing=relaxed groove, bpm 80-100, intensityCurve=steady, energy=medium-low.
  - Tension / warning / contrast: tracks with mood=tense/suspense, pacing=held or pulsing, intensityCurve=swelling, energy=medium.
  - Reveal / climax / payoff: tracks with mood=lift/triumphant/decisive, pacing=upbeat, intensityCurve=rising-or-peak, energy=high.
  - Resolution / cool-down / aftermath: tracks with mood=contemplative/warm-resolve, pacing=relaxed, intensityCurve=falling, energy=low to medium-low.
Music transitions between segments must be intentional, not crossfades by default. Choose one connector per transition: (a) crossfade 0.6-1.5s when the new track sits in a similar register, (b) suckback (250-500ms of music ducked to -16 dB) + reverse-tail riser into the new track on a downbeat reveal, (c) reverb tail on the outgoing segment + 200ms music gap + clean drop-in on the new section, (d) musical stinger or impact on the downbeat to land the change. Note the chosen connector in the segment rationale.
Music-only scoring beats are valid effects. When the picture wants emotional weight but no SFX, write rationale="music-carry" or rationale="silence-for-contrast" on the empty window. Tasteful silence between music beats is part of the score.
Plan a music-driven climax. Identify the single biggest emotional beat (the reveal, the thesis, the final lift) and design the surrounding music for it: music drop or impact on the downbeat, lower gainDb on the prior segment to make the climax segment 2-3 dB louder, and a clear musical resolution after.
Music gainDb is the literal relative gain. The renderer no longer multiplies it by any hidden musicVolume factor: a segment with gainDb="-10" lands at -10 dB relative to narration. Plan accordingly.
Music gain shaping (target range -13.5 to -5.5 dB): hook segment -8.5 to -6.5 dB; proof/build -9.5 to -7.5 dB; instruction sections -11.5 to -9.5 dB to let the voice breathe; climax/payoff segment -7.5 to -5.5 dB (the loudest segment of the score); resolution -10.5 to -8.5 dB. These targets are about 2.5 dB louder than the prior baseline while keeping narration primary. Do not run music flat across all segments. Aim for the music bus integrated LUFS to land within 4-8 LU below narration LUFS.
Loop-friendly tracks (loopFriendly=true in library JSON) can extend beyond their generated duration via stream loop. Tracks marked loopFriendly=false should not be looped; if the section is longer than the track, split it into two segments or pick a different track.
Narration owns the mix, but music must remain clearly audible. Plan musicDuckingDb between -6 and -4 (tasteful duck under speech). Plan musicDuckingUnderTransientsDb between -3 and -1 (light duck under transient SFX hits) -- it stacks per-cue, so a heavy SFX coverage with -4 dB duck will bury the music. Pair ducking with midrange EQ carving around 1.8-2.4 kHz so music sits beside the voice rather than under it. Duck music in the midrange around -6db to -8db. 
Music audibility QA target: the rendered music-only bus integrated LUFS should land within 12 LU of the narration+music (no-SFX) mix LUFS. If the bus is more than 12 LU below, the QA flags music-too-quiet and the design will need a higher gainDb or shallower duck.
Absolute minimum SFX gain floors (apply regardless of the music gain in that window): impacts >= -9 dB, clicks/ticks/taps >= -10 dB, risers >= -11 dB, whooshes >= -11 dB. These are about 3 dB quieter than the prior baseline (roughly 30% less amplitude) while still preserving audibility after mastering.
Relative-to-music gain rule (applied on top of the absolute floors above): transients should sit about 1 dB above the loudest music segment they overlap, while risers/whooshes may sit roughly even with the music when the music-under-transients sidechain is active. If music is at -10 dB, plan transients around -9 dB and risers/whooshes around -10 to -11 dB. Do NOT push transients far above music -- the music-under-transients sidechain already gives them headroom.
Ambience beds: -25 to -21 dB. Use one bed at a time. Layer a second only on tentpole beats.
SFX vs music balance check: before writing, sanity-check that the SFX bus would not be inaudible after final mastering. If you cannot point to at least 3 cues in different sections that would clearly bump the loudness meter above the music alone, raise gains or reduce ducking under those cues.
Captions, transcript, forced alignment, and visual timing are input context only. Do not time effects to caption boundaries by default. Tie cues to visual beats first, narration phrasing second, transcript word-by-word last.
Return compact timestamp-only XML inside <sound_design> with layered <track> tags and self-closing <effect /> tags.

Artifact requirements:
- Write YAML front matter first with title, status: needs review, date, agent: Scribe, and category: sound-design.
- After the front matter, write raw <sound_design> XML only.
- Use one <sound_design version="2" duckingDb="..." ambienceDuckingDb="..." motionDuckingDb="..." transientDuckingDb="..." transientBusGainDb="..." maxConcurrentOneShots="..." musicDuckingDb="..." musicEqCutDb="..." musicEqFrequencyHz="..." musicEqQ="..." outputSampleRate="..." outputChannels="..." masterLoudnessTargetLufs="..." masterTruePeakDb="..."> root element.
- Inside it, group layered audio lanes with <track id="..." role="..."> elements containing self-closing <effect /> cues.
- Optional music structure: add <music_segments> before/after the SFX tracks, with self-closing <segment id="..." trackId="saved-music-id" start="seconds" end="seconds" gainDb="..." fadeInMs="..." fadeOutMs="..." mood="..." pacing="..." rationale="..." /> entries. Use absolute timestamps only.
- Every effect must include id, type, and start="seconds". Include end="seconds" or duration="seconds" for beds/risers/tails when useful.
- Optional effect attrs: assetId, description, searchQuery, category, priority, gainDb, fadeInMs, fadeOutMs, groupId, frequencyBand, layerRole, stylePalette, literalness, rationale, overlap, musicDuckingDb, musicEqCutDb, musicEqFrequencyHz, musicEqQ, musicLowCutHz, musicHighCutHz.
- The first hook music segment should strongly prefer a dramatic, high-tension, cinematic, or magnetic saved track/stinger/score bed that grabs attention. It can transition into calmer proof or instruction music after the hook.
- Plan music gains in the -13.5 to -5.5 dB target range (gainDb is literal, no hidden multiplier): hook -8.5 to -6.5 dB; proof/build -9.5 to -7.5 dB; instruction -11.5 to -9.5 dB; climax/payoff -7.5 to -5.5 dB; resolution -10.5 to -8.5 dB. These values are about 2.5 dB louder than the prior baseline while keeping narration primary. Plan non-music SFX about 3 dB quieter than the prior baseline: impacts >= -9 dB, clicks/ticks/taps >= -10 dB, risers/whooshes >= -11 dB, ambience -25 to -21 dB; transients only need to sit about 1 dB above overlapping music and risers/whooshes may sit roughly even with it. musicDuckingDb -6 to -4. musicDuckingUnderTransientsDb -3 to -1 (it stacks per-cue).
- Stay within hard caps for a 60-90s short: at most 18 ticks/clicks/taps and at most 30 total <effect/> cues. Each cue must name a specific visual or narration beat in its rationale.
- Enforce asset rotation: no two consecutive cues of the same type may share the same assetId. Use the breadth of the library before reusing.
- Enforce leave-silence: in any 6s window of active narration, at least one >= 1.5s gap must have no transient cue.
- For tentpole beats (hook, thesis/reveal, mid-pivot, final payoff), plan a frequency-layered group with shared groupId covering low + mid + high, or mark the beat rationale="silence-for-contrast".
- Use whooshes only when the picture moves. If there is no visual motion, choose a tick, riser-tail, or silence instead.
- Risers must resolve into an impact, stinger, or music payoff within 100-300 ms of their end. A riser with no resolution is invalid.
- Placement must be timestamp-only. Use captions, transcript, word-level forced alignment, and visual timing as inputs, but do not emit anchors, sceneId, captionId, caption tags, scene references, or caption-boundary timing properties in the XML.
- Treat the visual beat map as first-class timing truth. Lock to scene cuts, reveals, zooms, camera moves, before/after comparisons, and graphic step-throughs before locking to narration words.
- Use the saved library as the allowed source palette. Match library metadata (style palette, frequency band, layer role, literalness) when present.
- Score the video like a film: write the emotional arc in the rationale of the first music segment, then use >= 2 music segments for videos > 20s (>= 3 for > 50s, >= 4 for > 75s).
- Pick music tracks by metadata (mood, pacing, bpm, key, energy, emotionalArc, intensityCurve, bestSceneTypes, comparableTo). Never pick by trackId name guessing.
- For every music segment transition, choose an intentional connector (crossfade, suckback + reverse riser, reverb-tail + gap + drop-in, or stinger landing) and name it in the segment rationale.
- Reserve at least one music-driven climax beat where music gain is 2-3 dB louder than the surrounding segments and SFX support (not replace) the lift.
- Write the updated artifact back to {{soundDesignPath}}, then read it back and verify the file exists and contains a <sound_design> root with <track> and <effect> entries.

Revision integrity details:
- Before writing {{soundDesignPath}}, read the existing file if present and compare only the body after YAML front matter.
- Do not rewrite or touch the artifact with body-identical sound-design XML. A timestamp/front-matter-only change is not a successful Plan Sound Design revision.
- If an existing sound-design XML body is present, make a meaningful cue, timing, gain, track, rationale, or music-bed revision that changes the XML body while preserving valid schema and QA constraints.
- If you cannot make a meaningful body change, fail clearly instead of writing the same body again.
- Existing artifact body state: {{existingSoundDesignBodySummary}}
revisionPromptTemplate:
Revision notes: {{revisionNotes}}
Keep the revised sound-design XML timestamp-only: no anchors, sceneId, captionId, scene references, or caption-boundary timing properties.

library.name: Soft organic hit
library.recommendedUses: Opening punctuation, subtle reveal landings, light emphasis beats.
library.avoidUses: Avoid stacking on every caption or every cut.
library.notes: Imported from original Freesound download. Gentle starter impact. Source: https://freesound.org/people/Markozz/sounds/334428/ · Creator: Markozz.

library.name: Deep bass hit
library.recommendedUses: Big reveals, dramatic pivots, before-and-after payoffs.
library.avoidUses: Avoid under dense narration or light comedic beats.
library.notes: Imported from original Freesound download. Low-end emphasis hit. Source: https://freesound.org/people/phluidbox/sounds/339437/ · Creator: phluidbox.

library.name: Smash hit short
library.recommendedUses: Fast-cut accents, text landings, abrupt visual punches.
library.avoidUses: Avoid on soft or contemplative edits.
library.notes: Imported from original Freesound download. Quick bright impact. Source: https://freesound.org/people/adh.dreaming/sounds/632511/ · Creator: adh.dreaming.

library.name: Pop accent
library.recommendedUses: Playful graphics, UI reveals, light punctuation.
library.avoidUses: Avoid heavy dramatic transitions.
library.notes: Imported from original Freesound download. Lighter pop-style impact. Source: https://freesound.org/people/yaplasut1843/sounds/517101/ · Creator: yaplasut1843.

library.name: UI click soft
library.recommendedUses: Button taps, on-screen toggles, subtle caption emphasis.
library.avoidUses: Avoid using on every word or every scene cut.
library.notes: Imported from original Freesound download. Soft UI tick. Source: https://freesound.org/people/finix473/sounds/546974/ · Creator: finix473.

library.name: UI button tick
library.recommendedUses: Tighter UI accents and quick editorial tick marks.
library.avoidUses: Avoid if you need a natural physical click.
library.notes: Imported from original Freesound download. Tight button tick. Source: https://freesound.org/people/el_boss/sounds/677861/ · Creator: el_boss.

library.name: Glitch click
library.recommendedUses: Tech graphics, glitches, digital overlays.
library.avoidUses: Avoid in organic or cinematic-only sections.
library.notes: Imported from original Freesound download. Digital glitch tick. Source: https://freesound.org/people/potentjello/sounds/194105/ · Creator: potentjello.

library.name: Micro click accent
library.recommendedUses: Tiny text pops, micro-confirmations, delicate UI feedback.
library.avoidUses: Avoid where a fuller transient is needed.
library.notes: Imported from original Freesound download. Very short micro click. Source: https://freesound.org/people/Magicturtle985/sounds/346126/ · Creator: Magicturtle985.

library.name: Whoosh short clean
library.recommendedUses: Clean transitions, fast graphic movement, swipe edits.
library.avoidUses: Avoid long dramatic reveals.
library.notes: Imported from original Freesound download. Short clean transition whoosh. Source: https://freesound.org/people/FartCTO/sounds/789840/ · Creator: FartCTO.

library.name: Whip zoom whoosh
library.recommendedUses: Whip pans, zoom transitions, aggressive motion edits.
library.avoidUses: Avoid still frames and soft pacing.
library.notes: Imported from original Freesound download. Designed for whip zoom motion. Source: https://freesound.org/people/BennettFilmTeacher/sounds/486234/ · Creator: BennettFilmTeacher.

library.name: Fast whip whoosh
library.recommendedUses: Punchier transitions and fast movement passes.
library.avoidUses: Avoid slow explanatory sections.
library.notes: Imported from original Freesound download. Faster movement-focused whip. Source: https://freesound.org/people/ErikCruzDev/sounds/735907/ · Creator: ErikCruzDev.

library.name: Transition woosh
library.recommendedUses: General-purpose scene transitions and motion accents.
library.avoidUses: Avoid if you already have multiple whooshes stacked on the cut.
library.notes: Imported from original Freesound download. Clean general-purpose woosh. Source: https://freesound.org/people/florianreichelt/sounds/683096/ · Creator: florianreichelt.

library.name: Simple tonal riser
library.recommendedUses: Short anticipation before reveals, title cards, or big cuts.
library.avoidUses: Avoid running under long narration blocks.
library.notes: Imported from original Freesound download. Short tonal anticipation riser. Source: https://freesound.org/people/SoundEffectsForAll/sounds/840719/ · Creator: SoundEffectsForAll.

library.name: Noisy riser build
library.recommendedUses: Tension builds and more aggressive transitions.
library.avoidUses: Avoid in soft lifestyle or calm explanatory sections.
library.notes: Imported from original Freesound download. Noisier suspense riser. Source: https://freesound.org/people/rubenrox/sounds/487698/ · Creator: rubenrox.

library.name: Long tension riser
library.recommendedUses: Longer build-ups before a major turn or payoff.
library.avoidUses: Avoid micro transitions or short captions.
library.notes: Imported from original Freesound download. Long suspense riser. Source: https://freesound.org/people/xkeril/sounds/789101/ · Creator: xkeril.

library.name: Uplifter riser
library.recommendedUses: Cinematic transitions and polished reveal setups.
library.avoidUses: Avoid when a naturalistic sound bed is the goal.
library.notes: Imported from original Freesound download. Cinematic uplifting riser. Source: https://freesound.org/people/InMotionAudio/sounds/676333/ · Creator: InMotionAudio.

library.name: Neutral room tone
library.recommendedUses: Dialogue cleanup, quiet sections, seamless background continuity.
library.avoidUses: Avoid as the only bed when you need a stylized or cinematic mood.
library.notes: Imported from original Freesound download. Neutral room tone bed. Source: https://freesound.org/people/Frederik_Sunne/sounds/274339/ · Creator: Frederik_Sunne.

library.name: Office ambience bed
library.recommendedUses: Interior explainer scenes, desk setups, work montage support.
library.avoidUses: Avoid outdoors or ultra-minimal dialogue moments.
library.notes: Imported from original Freesound download. Natural office bed. Source: https://freesound.org/people/DavidFrbr/sounds/327497/ · Creator: DavidFrbr.

library.name: Open field wind bed
library.recommendedUses: Outdoor scenes, spacious visuals, calm atmospheric support.
library.avoidUses: Avoid crowded indoor scenes.
library.notes: Imported from original Freesound download. Outdoor airy wind bed. Source: https://freesound.org/people/nickmaysoundmusic/sounds/523374/ · Creator: nickmaysoundmusic.

library.name: Soft tonal air bed 1
library.recommendedUses: Polished lifestyle edits, subtle designed atmosphere, sparse narration sections.
library.avoidUses: Avoid when strong music already fills the high end.
library.notes: Imported from original Freesound download. Designed soft tonal air texture. Source: https://freesound.org/people/janbezouska/sounds/397091/ · Creator: janbezouska.

library.name: Soft tonal air bed 2
library.recommendedUses: Alternate airy tonal texture for lighter cinematic beds.
library.avoidUses: Avoid if you already used bed 1 and want contrast.
library.notes: Imported from original Freesound download. Alternate soft tonal air texture. Source: https://freesound.org/people/janbezouska/sounds/397092/ · Creator: janbezouska.

library.name: Industrial air tone
library.recommendedUses: Tension beds, darker reveals, moody transitions.
library.avoidUses: Avoid bright upbeat content unless used very subtly.
library.notes: Imported from original Freesound download. Darker industrial air bed. Source: https://freesound.org/people/Geoff-Bremner-Audio/sounds/731434/ · Creator: Geoff-Bremner-Audio.

library.name: Airy texture bed
library.recommendedUses: General atmospheric lift under minimal sections and visual transitions.
library.avoidUses: Avoid when you need a naturalistic literal ambience.
library.notes: Imported from original Freesound download. Designed airy texture bed. Source: https://freesound.org/people/SakuShea/sounds/710930/ · Creator: SakuShea.

library.name: Trailer boom impact
library.recommendedUses: Big hook landings, product reveals, and bold section transitions that need low-end weight.
library.avoidUses: Avoid firing on every cut or under already-heavy kick-driven music.
library.notes: Imported from Freesound HQ preview because anonymous original downloads require login. Wide trailer-style boom with a longer cinematic tail. Source: https://freesound.org/people/harrisonlace/sounds/816376/ · Creator: harrisonlace.

library.name: Tight FX impact
library.recommendedUses: Quick visual reveals, stat callouts, and short punch-ins where a fast transient reads better than a boom.
library.avoidUses: Avoid using as a bed element or under delicate ambience-only scenes.
library.notes: Imported from Freesound HQ preview because anonymous original downloads require login. Fast synthetic impact that reads cleanly in short-form cuts. Source: https://freesound.org/people/Mo-reno-lopez/sounds/670852/ · Creator: Mo-reno-lopez.

library.name: Cartoon crack impact
library.recommendedUses: Playful reveals, meme beats, visual pop-ins, and lighter creator-style edits.
library.avoidUses: Avoid in serious or cinematic moments that want weight instead of brightness.
library.notes: Imported from Freesound HQ preview because anonymous original downloads require login. Bright cartoony crack that keeps impact moments energetic instead of heavy. Source: https://freesound.org/people/FFeller/sounds/532872/ · Creator: FFeller.

library.name: Bright stinger hit
library.recommendedUses: Sentence punctuation, quote pop-ins, and fast attention resets between ideas.
library.avoidUses: Avoid stacking with other bright clicks on the same beat.
library.notes: Imported from Freesound HQ preview because anonymous original downloads require login. Sharper bright hit that works well as a clean editorial stinger. Source: https://freesound.org/people/xkeril/sounds/736849/ · Creator: xkeril.

library.name: Sonar pulse hit
library.recommendedUses: Tech explainers, product UI reveals, and modern minimal transitions that want tone with the hit.
library.avoidUses: Avoid in organic or naturalistic scenes where synthetic tone feels out of place.
library.notes: Imported from Freesound HQ preview because anonymous original downloads require login. Minimal tech-forward hit with a slightly tonal sonar flavor. Source: https://freesound.org/people/xkeril/sounds/736851/ · Creator: xkeril.

library.name: Industrial hit wide
library.recommendedUses: Feature reveals, dashboard fly-ins, and bigger motion moments that need a designed hit with body.
library.avoidUses: Avoid overusing in lightweight conversational edits.
library.notes: Imported from Freesound HQ preview because anonymous original downloads require login. Broader designed impact with a slightly industrial texture and fuller tail. Source: https://freesound.org/people/vekoN/sounds/719831/ · Creator: vekoN.

library.name: UI click round
library.recommendedUses: Button confirms, lower-third pop-ins, and tidy caption emphasis.
library.avoidUses: Avoid combining with harsher clicks on the same syllable.
library.notes: Imported from Freesound HQ preview because anonymous original downloads require login. Rounded UI click that feels polished and neutral. Source: https://freesound.org/people/Ranner/sounds/488534/ · Creator: Ranner.

library.name: Button click snappy
library.recommendedUses: Quick list reveals, interface taps, and app-demo moments.
library.avoidUses: Avoid using repeatedly under dense subtitles.
library.notes: Imported from Freesound HQ preview because anonymous original downloads require login. Snappy button click with a clean attack and short decay. Source: https://freesound.org/people/suntemple/sounds/253168/ · Creator: suntemple.

library.name: Menu click confirm
library.recommendedUses: Selection changes, menu screenshots, and playful CTA moments.
library.avoidUses: Avoid in premium cinematic edits where a game-menu tone feels too literal.
library.notes: Imported from Freesound HQ preview because anonymous original downloads require login. Slightly more characterful menu click that still stays usable for UI accents. Source: https://freesound.org/people/jackosnb/sounds/736912/ · Creator: jackosnb.

library.name: Minimal dry click
library.recommendedUses: Small caption ticks, chart step-throughs, and restrained UI motion.
library.avoidUses: Avoid when you need a clearly branded or glossy UI sound.
library.notes: Imported from Freesound HQ preview because anonymous original downloads require login. Dry minimal click that stays subtle in dialogue-heavy scenes. Source: https://freesound.org/people/cabled_mess/sounds/370962/ · Creator: cabled_mess.

library.name: Sharp click accent
library.recommendedUses: Fast text accents, number changes, and beat-matched subtitle emphasis.
library.avoidUses: Avoid in soft ambient sections where bright ticks poke out too much.
library.notes: Imported from Freesound HQ preview because anonymous original downloads require login. Sharper click for high-definition micro accents. Source: https://freesound.org/people/TriqyStudio/sounds/653369/ · Creator: TriqyStudio.

library.name: Atonal high click
library.recommendedUses: Precision highlights, timeline markers, and technical callouts where a crisp high tick helps focus.
library.avoidUses: Avoid overusing near harsh narration sibilance.
library.notes: Imported from Freesound HQ preview because anonymous original downloads require login. Very short high click that feels precise rather than friendly. Source: https://freesound.org/people/lennartgreen/sounds/566888/ · Creator: lennartgreen.

library.name: Futuristic pass-by whoosh
library.recommendedUses: Device transitions, bold scene wipes, and product UI motion.
library.avoidUses: Avoid on every camera cut, it is more noticeable than a subtle swish.
library.notes: Imported from Freesound HQ preview because anonymous original downloads require login. Designed futuristic whoosh with a clear pass-by contour. Source: https://freesound.org/people/JW_Audio/sounds/848212/ · Creator: JW_Audio.

library.name: Little air swish
library.recommendedUses: Small motion graphics, emoji fly-ins, and quick object moves.
library.avoidUses: Avoid when the visual move is big enough to want a fuller whoosh.
library.notes: Imported from Freesound HQ preview because anonymous original downloads require login. Small light swish that works for micro transitions. Source: https://freesound.org/people/ch_ase/sounds/423799/ · Creator: ch_ase.

library.name: Clean FX whoosh
library.recommendedUses: General edit transitions, timeline jumps, and composited motion beats.
library.avoidUses: Avoid layering with another full-range whoosh unless you really want density.
library.notes: Imported from Freesound HQ preview because anonymous original downloads require login. General-purpose clean whoosh with enough body for most editorial transitions. Source: https://freesound.org/people/ScythicBlade/sounds/802119/ · Creator: ScythicBlade.

library.name: Medium swoosh
library.recommendedUses: Topic changes, B-roll pans, and general swipe transitions.
library.avoidUses: Avoid under dense music where the midrange sweep can disappear or muddy.
library.notes: Imported from Freesound HQ preview because anonymous original downloads require login. Broader editorial swoosh that sits between subtle swishes and hero whooshes. Source: https://freesound.org/people/MasterEngine/sounds/418007/ · Creator: MasterEngine.

library.name: Swing arc swoosh
library.recommendedUses: Object tosses, chart sweeps, and more physical motion beats.
library.avoidUses: Avoid for futuristic UI motion where a natural swing sound feels too literal.
library.notes: Imported from Freesound HQ preview because anonymous original downloads require login. A more physical arc-shaped swoosh that sells movement. Source: https://freesound.org/people/Cornwallis89/sounds/244598/ · Creator: Cornwallis89.

library.name: Reverse backspin whoosh
library.recommendedUses: Pre-reveal pulls, anticipation beats, and reverse transitions into a new idea.
library.avoidUses: Avoid using right on the landing when a forward-moving whoosh would read better.
library.notes: Imported from Freesound HQ preview because anonymous original downloads require login. Backward-feeling sweep that creates anticipation before a reveal. Source: https://freesound.org/people/SlavicMagic/sounds/446010/ · Creator: SlavicMagic.

library.name: Classic bounce riser
library.recommendedUses: Lead-ins to a hook payoff, headline stat reveal, or strong section button.
library.avoidUses: Avoid under every scene change or the pacing starts to feel over-produced.
library.notes: Imported from Freesound HQ preview because anonymous original downloads require login. Classic energetic riser that clearly telegraphs an incoming landing. Source: https://freesound.org/people/johnjohnfm/sounds/832275/ · Creator: johnjohnfm.

library.name: Mid-length build riser
library.recommendedUses: General anticipation before a title card, list climax, or proof point.
library.avoidUses: Avoid in quiet documentary-style moments that want restraint.
library.notes: Imported from Freesound HQ preview because anonymous original downloads require login. Neutral mid-length riser that is easy to fit into many short-form builds. Source: https://freesound.org/people/reathance/sounds/503818/ · Creator: reathance.

library.name: Long suspense riser alt
library.recommendedUses: Longer reveals, before-and-after setups, and delayed payoffs with sustained tension.
library.avoidUses: Avoid on fast punchy edits where the build outlasts the visual beat.
library.notes: Imported from Freesound HQ preview because anonymous original downloads require login. Longer suspense build for edits that want a slower, more dramatic rise. Source: https://freesound.org/people/xkeril/sounds/789102/ · Creator: xkeril.

library.name: Clean uplifter
library.recommendedUses: Positive reveals, wins, testimonials, and upbeat product moments.
library.avoidUses: Avoid in ominous or high-threat moments that want darker tension.
library.notes: Imported from Freesound HQ preview because anonymous original downloads require login. Cleaner more optimistic uplifter that reads as momentum instead of dread. Source: https://freesound.org/people/digit-al/sounds/268559/ · Creator: digit-al.

library.name: Glitch tension riser
library.recommendedUses: Tech explainers, bug reveals, AI-themed cuts, and digital tension moments.
library.avoidUses: Avoid in warm natural lifestyle edits where glitch texture distracts.
library.notes: Imported from Freesound HQ preview because anonymous original downloads require login. Digital-glitch flavored riser for more synthetic tension builds. Source: https://freesound.org/people/johnjohnfm/sounds/806666/ · Creator: johnjohnfm.

library.name: Tonal wave riser
library.recommendedUses: Softer anticipation, aesthetic beauty moments, and polished motion graphics.
library.avoidUses: Avoid if you need a noisy or aggressive rise with obvious tension.
library.notes: Imported from Freesound HQ preview because anonymous original downloads require login. More tonal polished riser for elegant, less abrasive builds. Source: https://freesound.org/people/adh.dreaming/sounds/572636/ · Creator: adh.dreaming.

library.name: Room tone mono bed
library.recommendedUses: Talking-head scenes, room-based B-roll, and anywhere narration needs a subtle sense of space.
library.avoidUses: Avoid using too loud, it should support presence rather than compete with speech.
library.notes: Imported from Freesound HQ preview because anonymous original downloads require login. Neutral room-tone bed for natural interior grounding. Source: https://freesound.org/people/Julien_Matthey/sounds/837886/ · Creator: Julien_Matthey.

library.name: Open office bed
library.recommendedUses: Workspace montages, startup scenes, and collaboration visuals that benefit from a lived-in office feel.
library.avoidUses: Avoid under exposed dialogue if the background chatter becomes distracting.
library.notes: Imported from Freesound HQ preview because anonymous original downloads require login. Office-flavored ambience bed with more activity than a plain room tone. Source: https://freesound.org/people/Chelly01/sounds/541117/ · Creator: Chelly01.

library.name: Soft wind ambience
library.recommendedUses: Outdoor cutaways, aerials, landscape motion, and reflective pacing breaks.
library.avoidUses: Avoid under very bass-heavy music where the airy texture disappears.
library.notes: Imported from Freesound HQ preview because anonymous original downloads require login. Soft outdoor wind bed for open-air scenes and breathing space. Source: https://freesound.org/people/haniebal/sounds/423314/ · Creator: haniebal.

library.name: Tech alien ambience
library.recommendedUses: AI demos, futuristic dashboards, cybersecurity visuals, and abstract tech montages.
library.avoidUses: Avoid in naturalistic office scenes where the sci-fi tone feels too designed.

library.name: Dark drone ambience
library.recommendedUses: Problem setup, stakes-building sections, and more dramatic explanatory beats.

library.notes: Imported from Freesound HQ preview because anonymous original downloads require login. Low dark drone bed for more serious or suspenseful sections. Source: https://freesound.org/people/Bowesy/sounds/691481/ · Creator: Bowesy.

library.name: Editorial glass tick
library.recommendedUses: Premium beauty/editorial stat ticks, before-after reveals, glassy confirmation accents, and polished caption emphasis.
library.avoidUses: Avoid stacking with other bright ticks on sibilant narration or every caption beat.
library.notes: Imported from Freesound HQ preview because anonymous original downloads require login. Short glass/ceramic tick with a premium editorial transient. Source: https://freesound.org/people/SamsterBirdies/sounds/745291/ · Creator: SamsterBirdies.

library.name: Tight wooden tap
library.recommendedUses: Organic/tactile emphasis, human-feeling step-throughs, list progression, and grounded micro beats.
library.avoidUses: Avoid where the visual language is purely digital or glossy tech.

library.name: Soft digital blip click

library.name: Low mechanical tick

library.name: Short spark uplift

library.notes: Imported from Freesound HQ preview because anonymous original downloads require login. Short crystal/chime transition that works as a sparkly uplift. Source: https://freesound.org/people/newlocknew/sounds/773403/ · Creator: newlocknew. Curated on 2026-04-30: trimmed/normalized from CC0 Freesound source for predictable short-form audibility.

library.name: Mid pulse build
library.recommendedUses: Mid-energy section turns, proof-to-payoff builds, and rhythmic transitions needing body without a huge trailer swell.
library.avoidUses: Avoid firing repeatedly on adjacent 1.6s cuts; save for argument turns.
library.notes: Imported from Freesound HQ preview because anonymous original downloads require login. Modern riser-hit transition with enough body for section momentum. Source: https://freesound.org/people/AudioPapkin/sounds/822420/ · Creator: AudioPapkin. Curated on 2026-04-30: trimmed/normalized from CC0 Freesound source for predictable short-form audibility.

library.name: Soft noise swell
library.recommendedUses: Airy pre-lifts into visual transitions, soft anticipation, and narration-safe scene changes.

library.notes: Imported from Freesound HQ preview because anonymous original downloads require login. Short reverse white-noise whoosh usable as a soft swell. Source: https://freesound.org/people/HowardV/sounds/402818/ · Creator: HowardV. Curated on 2026-04-30: trimmed/normalized from CC0 Freesound source for predictable short-form audibility.

library.name: Micro reverse tick riser

library.notes: Imported from Freesound HQ preview because anonymous original downloads require login. Short reverse/suction transition for precise pre-beat pull. library.name: Modern UI snap click

library.recommendedUses: Crisp menu-style click for stat reveals, checklist beats, and short transitions.

library.recommendedUses: Tactile organic click for instruction beats, physical-action emphasis, and warm editorial palettes.

library.name: Wet tap click
library.recommendedUses: Wet/playful UI tap for fun reveals and pop-style emphasis. Adds character to the click bank.

library.name: White-noise reverse swell
library.recommendedUses: Short reverse swell into reveals, drops, and section turns. Resolves into an impact.
library.avoidUses: Avoid using without a resolving impact, stinger, or music payoff.
library.notes: Imported from Freesound HQ preview because anonymous original downloads require login. Atmospheric white-noise swell building to a climax with light reverb, ideal as a suckback before a reveal. Source: https://freesound.org/people/HowardV/sounds/402818/ · Creator: HowardV.

library.name: Reverse crash cymbal
library.recommendedUses: Reverse cymbal swell for tentpole reveals and section turns. Use the last 1-3 seconds before the downbeat with duration to clip it.
library.avoidUses: Avoid on every transition - reserve for hooks, thesis reveals, and mid-pivots.
library.notes: Imported from Freesound HQ preview because anonymous original downloads require login. Reversed crash cymbal with delay, classic EDM-style sweep into impact. Source: https://freesound.org/people/Cloud-10/sounds/677710/ · Creator: Cloud-10.

library.name: Organic noise build riser
library.recommendedUses: Three-second build into reveals, drops, or section turns when a longer build is needed.
library.avoidUses: Avoid pairing with another riser overlapping in the same window.
library.notes: Imported from Freesound HQ preview because anonymous original downloads require login. Organic noise ascending build designed to add excitement and suspense. Source: https://freesound.org/people/syntheffects/sounds/685256/ · Creator: syntheffects.

library.name: Lunar short uplifter
library.recommendedUses: Two-second uplift for stat reveals, fast-twitch beats, and proof-section turns.
library.avoidUses: Avoid in slow cinematic sections - this is paced for energetic editorial.
library.notes: Imported from Freesound HQ preview because anonymous original downloads require login. Brief rising electronic sound effect, energetic and bright. Source: https://freesound.org/people/maqsim/sounds/172148/ · Creator: maqsim.

library.name: Smooth sub drop
library.recommendedUses: Low-end weight under reveals and tentpole beats. Layer with a mid-body impact and high air tick.
library.avoidUses: Avoid stacking with another sub drop in the same 4-second window.
library.notes: Imported from Freesound HQ preview because anonymous original downloads require login. Smooth descending bass drop adding sub-frequency weight to reveals. Source: https://freesound.org/people/newagesoup/sounds/428073/ · Creator: newagesoup.

library.name: Cinematic low slam impact
library.recommendedUses: Sustained low-end impact for final-beat payoff or tentpole reveal with a fade tail.
library.avoidUses: Avoid in fast-cut sections where the long fade tail competes with the next cue.
library.notes: Imported from Freesound HQ preview because anonymous original downloads require login. Gentle cinematic low-pitch impact with a slam that gradually fades out. Source: https://freesound.org/people/Jofae/sounds/408141/ · Creator: Jofae.

library.name: Deep cinematic woosh
library.recommendedUses: Long cinematic sweep for major scene-to-scene transitions when the picture moves substantially.
library.avoidUses: Avoid as a generic transition - reserve for actual whip pans, camera moves, or large graphic sweeps.
library.notes: Imported from Freesound HQ preview because anonymous original downloads require login. Deep impactful whoosh recorded with professional field gear, suited for cinematic transitions. Source: https://freesound.org/people/AudioPapkin/sounds/648538/ · Creator: AudioPapkin.

library.name: Game-up stinger
library.recommendedUses: Positive-direction stinger on stat reveals, completion beats, and good-news payoffs.
library.avoidUses: Avoid on warning or negative beats - direction reads as too positive.
library.notes: Imported from Freesound HQ preview because anonymous original downloads require login. Short punchy game-style notification that captures attention without being jarring. Source: https://freesound.org/people/Fupicat/sounds/538149/ · Creator: Fupicat.

library.name: Buzz-down stinger
library.recommendedUses: Negative-direction stinger on warning beats, wrong-form callouts, and contrast moments.
library.avoidUses: Avoid as a payoff - it reads as a fail or warning.
library.notes: Imported from Freesound HQ preview because anonymous original downloads require login. Short harsh descending buzz suitable for quiz wrong-answer or pass-fail contrast beats. Source: https://freesound.org/people/Beetlemuse/sounds/692847/ · Creator: Beetlemuse.
